title: Patriots reportedly cut linebacker Jahlani Tavai
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots released linebacker Jahlani Tavai after five seasons, following a decline in his role in 2025.
Tavai had back-to-back 110+ tackle seasons in 2023-2024, but recorded only 42 tackles in 12 games in 2025.
Tavai overcame health issues and personal tragedy in 2025, including an autoimmune disease and the loss of a child.

title: RFK Jr. blasted Dunkin’ for selling sugary drinks. Which menu items fit his criteria?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: RFK Jr. criticizes Dunkin' for selling drinks with high sugar content, questioning their safety for young consumers.
Dunkin's menu features 40 drinks exceeding 115 grams of sugar, some nearing 172 grams in large sizes.
American Heart Association recommends men limit sugar to 36g/day, while Dunkin's drinks significantly exceed this guideline.

title: Two Mass. lottery players win big on new scratch ticket games
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jose Fernandes of Woburn won $2.6 million from a $4 million prize on the 'Cash Doubler' scratch ticket.
Yancy Contreras Menjivar from Nantucket won $1.3 million in the 'Staked' game, buying the ticket at Old South Diner.
The Melrose Mobil and Old South Diner received $40,000 and $20,000 bonuses for selling the winning lottery tickets.

title: ‘Goals are worth chasing’: Why this single mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Kimberly Long, a single mom from Las Vegas, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to inspire her son.
Long began running during COVID-19, evolving from a 5K goal to marathons, highlighting persistence and heart.
She is part of the Dana-Farber Marathon Challenge, raising funds for cancer research in honor of a friend's daughter.

title: PHOTOS: This was the scene at Sunday’s Shamrock Splash into Boston Harbor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Hundreds participated in the 16th Annual Shamrock Splash at Constitution Beach, raising funds for the Better Beaches Grant Program.
Save the Harbor/Save the Bay and JetBlue hosted the event, supporting diverse community events on Boston Harbor beaches.
Last year's grants enabled nearly 200 summer events, engaging diverse communities in Boston's waterfront activities.

title: ‘We’re not concerned about Boston’: Pistons’ head coach referenced Celtics amid four-game losing streak
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Detroit Pistons face a four-game losing streak, narrowing their Eastern Conference lead over the Boston Celtics to 2.5 games.
Pistons head coach J.B. Bickerstaff stated his team isn't worried about the Celtics, focusing on resolving their own issues.
Detroit, at 45-18, plays Brooklyn next; Celtics, 43-21, face the Spurs and defending champion Thunder in upcoming games.

title: A Mass. destination makes an excellent Memorial Day weekend trip, according to Conde Nast Traveler
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Conde Nast Traveler names Cape Cod a top destination for Memorial Day weekend 2026.
The publication highlights whale watching and local food as Cape Cod attractions during the holiday.
Recommended accommodations include The Villages at Ocean Edge Resort and Chatham Bars Inn.

title: Boston’s Patricia Cornwell sees her books land on screen with ‘Scarpetta’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Patricia Cornwell's 'Scarpetta' series is adapted into a TV show starring Nicole Kidman, streaming on Prime Video March 11.
Cornwell plays a cameo as a judge in the series, which includes Jamie Lee Curtis and Bobby Cannavale as part of the cast.
Cornwell's memoir 'True Crime' releasing May 2 explores her personal struggles and is inspired by her series adaptation journey.

title: Patriots free agency live updates: Pats sign edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots have signed edge rusher Dre'Mont Jones to a 3-year, $39 million contract.
The Patriots signed fullback Reggie Gilliam to a 3-year deal worth up to $12 million, with $6 million guaranteed.
Former Patriots tight end Austin Hooper signed a 1-year, $3.25 million deal with the Atlanta Falcons.

title: Boston Marathon runner aims to complete marathons in all 50 states
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Zara Harding, 46, aims to run marathons in all 50 states, with 10 states remaining after Boston.
Harding, from Henderson, Nevada, resumed marathon running post cancer recovery, achieving her personal best in 2023.
She's part of the Dana-Farber Marathon Challenge, raising $12,000 for cancer research at the Claudia Adams Barr Program.
